DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS TAYLOR FREEZERS OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., DOUGLAS NELSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL WITHIN A MUTUALLY AGREED UPON TIME PERIOD, PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF OBJECTING AT TRIAL TO THE ADMISSIBILITY OF ANY SUCH DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM THOMAS COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Arnold J. Berschler, Esq., Breschler Attorney at Law,. Telephone 800-338-1441, appearing for Plaintiff;
Samlecia Gaye, Esq., Gordon & Rees, Telephone 415-875-3390, appearing for Defendant Taylor Freezers et al;

RULING - ARGUED: The Court modifies its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS TAYLOR FREEZERS OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., DOUGLAS NELSON'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses.

The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall within a mutually agreed upon time period, provide code-compliant responses to the requested discovery. This order is without prejudice to Plaintiff objecting at trial to the admissibility of any such discovery. Prevailing party is to prepare and submit proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Thomas Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
